name: DAICHI SHOKUJOU NO JUTSU (EARTHEN MACE TECHNIQUE)

description.value:
Type: Ninjutsu (Doton)

Rank: 5

Chakra Cost: 6

Learn DC: 18 | Successes: 3

Perform: 7 | Perform DC: 18

Components: C, F, Mas

This technique was developed by Doton Ninjutsu users to increase their lethal efficiency in melee combat. By forcing chakra into his Quarter Staff, the user thrusts it into the ground beneath him, the staff seeming to cut into the earth with ease. The user then sends a blast of chakra into the ground around it, breaking apart the earth near the staff. He then attaches the loose earth to the staff as he pulls it back out, creating a large, strong mace with which he can strike his enemies. All this is done in one quick, fluid motion. The hammer deals 1d10

earth and 1d10 bludgeoning damage plus Strength modifier. After the duration of the technique ends, the user can maintain it as a free action at the cost of 3 more chakra. If the user completely releases the staff for any amount of time, the chakra he is using to hold the earth to his staff dissipates, and the earth falls off. Performing this technique provokes an attack of opportunity.

Note: The second attack of a quarter staff cannot be used while this technique is in effect. The weapon is now considered to be a modified Great mace, although only the Nin Weapons Proficiency feat is required to wield it.

Requires: Ninjutsu 8 ranks

Material Focus: A small (at least 20 lbs.) quantity of earth, a quarter staff.

Mastery: With the 3rd level of mastery, the user gains a competency bonus to the attack roll equal to his Int. modifier. At the 5th level of mastery, this bonus is applied to both attack and damage.
